reasoning: A 10x increase above the baseline of 33.5 events means over 335 recorded ACLED events of 'Violence against civilians' would be required in a 30-day period. Because ACLED aggregates violence by location and date (meaning multiple fatalities in one village on one day constitute a single event), registering >335 events would require widespread, catastrophic violence occurring multiple times per day across many different locations. Recent reporting from January and February 2026 notes some localized clashes (e.g., between government forces and Azandé Ani Kpi Gbé in Haut-Mbomou) but entirely lacks evidence of a massive, unprecedented spike in targeted violence against civilians. For the first resolution date (Feb 22), 23 of the 30 days have already passed without major headlines, placing the probability effectively at the minimum allowed bound (0.02). The second resolution period (Feb 15 - Mar 17) has more temporal uncertainty, justifying a conservatively increased probability (0.03) in line with the forecaster's instructions, though it remains highly improbable that such extreme conditions will materialize.
